# Planning Brief - 2026-06-13

Source research: `.github/research/2026-06-13-research.md`

## Context (read this first)

The research brief flagged **four** mechanically transferable
upstream items in the last 48h:

1. cline #11480 - head+tail tool-output truncation (~4h, tool)
2. cline #11463 - strip command echo from `bash`/`shell` results
   (~2h, tool)
3. opencode #32074 - prefer MCP `structuredContent` over `content`
   (~3h, mcp)
4. opencode #30676 - optional `cwd` on local stdio MCP servers
   (~2h, mcp)

Plus one operational recommendation (queue-empty watchdog on Daily
Merge) and one rotation recommendation (demote `claude-code` /
`aider` from daily to weekly polling).

When today's planning cycle started, the auto-implement queue had:

- #768 covering research item #1 (head+tail) - correctly labelled.
- #769 covering Recommendation #1 (queue-empty watchdog) -
  correctly labelled.
- #765 covering research item #2 (strip command echo) - **already
  closed** (`assertNoCommandEcho` shipped 2026-06-13 09:08 UTC,
  see `src/tool/tools/bash.ts:217`).
- #766 covering research item #4 (MCP `cwd`) - **filed but missing
  the `auto-implement` label**. Engineering pipeline could not see
  it.
- #767 covering research item #3 (MCP `structuredContent`) - same
  bug, **missing the `auto-implement` label**.

This is the **third consecutive cycle** with the same labelling bug
(06-11 hit #754/#755/#756, 06-12 was a clean meta-fix cycle, 06-13
hit #766/#767). The 2026-06-12 plan asked `role-management` to
investigate the root cause; that has not landed. The right action
today is therefore the same shape as 2026-06-12: relabel orphans,
file the genuinely new actionable items, and — because the bug
recurred even after the meta-investigation request — escalate the
fix to a workflow-level guard rather than another root-cause memo.

## State at start of cycle

- Open `auto-implement` issues before this run: **2** (#768, #769).
- Open `enhancement` issues missing `auto-implement` (the orphans):
  **2** (#766, #767).
- Closed `auto-implement` issues today: **1** (#765 - command echo
  strip, shipped before this run).
- Engineering-agent runs since the 06-12 plan: ran on 2 issues,
  starved on the other 4 because of the orphan label bug.

| 0 | Add `auto-implement` label          | #766, #767         | Unblocks ~5h of MCP work pre-specified earlier today  |

| 2 | Auto-apply `auto-implement` label to issues created by Agent 2 planning        | ci     | small ~3h | `.github/workflows/agent2-planning.yml`                                                                           | #773  |

Total new issues: **2**. Combined with the two relabel fixes, the
post-cycle open `auto-implement` queue is **6** (#766, #767, #768,
#769, #772, #773).

| #3 MCP `structuredContent` (`mcp`, ~3h)        | #767             | Body complete, only the missing label was the problem; fixed via relabel above.  |

## Priority order for engineering pickup

Use the smallest-PR ladder the research brief recommended (compose
small wins first; #767 was deliberately ordered before #768 because
the head+tail budget is more useful once unrelated bytes - including
echo'd commands and structured-content fallbacks - aren't in the
way):

1. **#772** (config, ~30m) - one-file prompt edit, zero code surface.
2. **#766** (mcp, ~2h) - 5-line `spawn` option + 5 tests.
3. **#767** (mcp, ~3h) - 1 branch in `client.ts:callTool` + 6 tests.
4. **#773** (ci, ~3h) - workflow-only, gates the recurring labelling
   bug. Land before tomorrow's planning cycle to break the streak.
5. **#768** (tools, ~4h) - the largest LOC change. Compose with #765
   already shipped.
6. **#769** (ci, ~3h) - guardrail, useful but not blocking.

Total ready-to-implement effort visible after this cycle: **~15.5h**.
That comfortably exceeds the engineering-agent matrix (3 parallel
runs every 30 minutes) and should keep the queue warm through the
2026-06-15 cycle.

## Out-of-scope (no-action) signals from the brief

- claude-code: 0 functional commits in 48h (CHANGELOG bot only).
  Demoted to weekly polling via #772.
- aider: only model-id registrations for Anthropic 4.6/4.7. Not
  portable to SAP AI Core IDs. Demoted to weekly polling via #772.
- cline #11514: prompt nudge - per the research brief, "defer to
  Agent 2 audit". Not actioned today; will resurface in the next
  research cycle if it remains relevant.

## Recommendations forwarded to other roles

- **`role-management`**: the labelling bug recurred today (third
  cycle). The 2026-06-12 plan already asked for a root-cause
  investigation of the planning agent's `gh issue create` call.
  Until that lands, **#773 is the workflow-level safety net**; it
  does not replace the prompt-level fix. Please confirm whether
  the 2026-06-12 investigation produced any findings, and either
  close that ask or surface a follow-up issue.
- **`role-quality`**: when reviewing the four already-filed items
  (#766, #767, #768, #769), please sanity-check the `Done when`
  paths against `src/` HEAD, since #766 and #767 were drafted
  earlier today and the tree may have moved.
- **`role-engineering`**: pick #772 first (30 min, prompt-only),
  then #766 / #767 (composable MCP changes, can land in one PR if
  helpful).

## Carry-forward for the next research cycle

- Verify by 2026-06-14 morning that **none** of the new open
  `auto-implement` issues are stuck in `enhancement`-only orphan
  state. If #773 has merged, the `verify-labels` job should report
  zero corrections needed. If #773 has not merged but a fresh
  orphan appears, apply the relabel by hand and escalate to
  `role-management` immediately.
- Verify the 2026-06-13 `daily-merge-prs` run picked up the now-
  warm queue. If the engineering-agent cron still shows starvation
  in the next 24h, the bug is deeper than labelling.
- Track whether `claude-code` / `aider` (now weekly per #772, once
  it lands) produce any actionable item on Mon 2026-06-15. If yes,
  the rotation is justified. If still 0, consider dropping them
  entirely in the brief after that.
